title: Event Bus Durable Queues DLQ Redrive Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the event bus durable queues dlq redrive capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: The capability publishes a pending event, deduplicates it by organisation, event type, and caller-supplied idempotency key, lets a worker claim and process it to processed, retries a failing handler with bounded backoff up to maxAttempts, then transitions the record to dead_letter; an operator redrive re-enqueues a dead-lettered event as a fresh pending event under a new idempotency key. Canonical product events carry strict, versioned payload contracts (for example tenant.config.changed keyed on key and report.run keyed on reportId) while the generic event-bus path also backs test-only fixture events excluded from product readiness. Provider failure degrades readiness rather than fabricating successful delivery.

stateModel.description: Persistent state is the platform_events table partitioned by organisationId, holding eventType, idempotencyKey, payload, status in pending|processing|processed|dead_letter, and attempts against maxAttempts, alongside worker_heartbeats recording alive|stale|stopped liveness. Dead-letter records retain tenant, event type, attempt count, a payload summary, and the last error so redrive can reconstruct context; ordering is guaranteed per organisation and idempotency key with no cross-tenant ordering guarantee. Row-level security scopes every read and mutation to the owning organisation.

permissions.description: Routes are global, operator-only platform surfaces: reading the event list and the dead-letter list requires platform.events.read, redrive requires platform.events.write, and worker listing requires platform.workers.read. Tenant isolation is enforced by organisationId partitioning plus row-level security so one organisation can never observe or redrive another organisation's events, and list routes require an explicit organisationId. Secrets and provider credentials never cross the event boundary.

contracts.description: The external operator contract is GET /api/admin/events, GET /api/admin/events/dead-letter, and POST /api/admin/events/:eventId/redrive, with publishing and worker processing exposed only as server-internal use-case contracts behind the EventBusPort and its PostgresEventBusAdapter. Schema changes to canonical events require a new schemaVersion, backwards-compatible consumers, and migration/redrive compatibility evidence before promotion. The port/adapter seam keeps the persistence provider swappable without changing the operation semantics.

validation.description: Publish rejects payload fields that carry secrets, preserves the tenant id, and deduplicates repeated idempotency keys; list routes require organisationId and return 400 when it is missing, and redrive rejects an invalid or unknown event id with 400. Row-level security additionally hides foreign-tenant events so validation cannot be bypassed by id guessing. Invariants are exercised by the event-bus and event-redrive runtime proofs.

errorModel.description: Outcomes are deterministic and fail closed: a duplicate publish is deduplicated, a handler that exhausts maxAttempts is dead-lettered, a missing organisationId returns 400, an invalid redrive id returns 400, and an unavailable Postgres substrate yields an honest skip or readiness failure rather than a fabricated success. Errors are typed and logged with organisationId when tenant-scoped, and no failure path echoes secret-bearing payload fields.

auditModel.description: Operator redrive emits an event.redriven audit record under audit-before-change semantics, so the audit write precedes the state mutation. Event publication and worker processing are captured as durable operational records and worker heartbeats rather than as user-mutation audit events, and redrive audit excludes the underlying payload. This separation keeps the operational telemetry distinct from the privileged-action audit trail.

readinessModel.description: Readiness combines operator visibility of the event and dead-letter queues with worker heartbeat liveness (alive|stale|stopped); it degrades explicitly and disables mutation/side-effect paths that depend on the Postgres adapter while keeping read-only readiness and audit visible. Live proofs fail closed on row-level-security, secret-payload, retry, redrive, or heartbeat regressions and skip honestly when Postgres is unavailable, so production-shaped readiness is never reported as production-live.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by three runtime proofs: event-bus validates live Postgres outbox persistence, row-level security, and secret-payload rejection; event-worker validates processing, retry, dead-letter, and heartbeat; event-redrive validates the live admin routes and requeue, with the corpus describing this as level-4 live substrate and route validation. The proofs are the lineage evidence, not a USF readiness claim. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
